extra_model_fields.notes_for_reviewer: Prioritize verifying the provided CSV reproduces the normalized_score values for R1..R4 and that the FORMULAS sheet points to the correct embedded_packet input cells. If the CSV reproduces the numbers, absence of openpyxl parsing in the preview environment is acceptable.
extra_model_fields.recommended_priority: fix formula provenance first (c1,c2,c4), then metadata consistency (c5), then documentation polish (c3).

FORMULAS worksheet exists with cell-level formula examples referencing embedded_packet
computed_columns.csv present and reproduces normalized_score values
Submission metadata contains consistent final_score/passed flags

acceptance_criteria: release_decision.md contains a clear formula expression and a worked numeric example.
No 'needs_review' marker remains for normalized_score formula.
actions: Write the mathematical formula used to compute normalized risk contribution (e.g., per-driver normalization mapping to 0-100, aggregation rule such as max of driver contributions, and any caps or scaling factors).
Provide a short worked example applying the formula to one record (show numeric inputs -> intermediate values -> normalized_score).
Remove 'needs_review' flag for the normalized_score formula in the MD and replace with 'explicit formula supplied'.
planned_changes.description: Add explicit normalized_score formula to release_decision.md.

acceptance_criteria: FORMULAS sheet exists and lists cell formulas and provenance references.
computed_columns.csv is included in artifacts and contains intermediate columns matching the XLSX.
actions: Add a worksheet named 'FORMULAS' that contains: textual formula(s), explanation of each term, and cell address examples.
Ensure the per-record normalized_score column in the main sheet is produced by formula cells that reference embedded_packet input cells (no hard-coded constants).
Save the XLSX in a standard format; also export a tab/CSV named 'computed_columns.csv' with one row per record and all intermediate computed columns used to reach normalized_score.
planned_changes.description: Make XLSX provenance explicit and previewable.

acceptance_criteria: All constants used in formulas are documented with provenance or explicit justification.
Any remaining discretionary choices are labeled 'needs_expert_review' with clear justification.
actions: In release_decision.md and the FORMULAS sheet, list any constants (e.g., min/max ranges, caps), why they were chosen, and cite the embedded_packet or task brief if the value came from there.
If any discretionary assumption was necessary, mark it explicitly and label it for 'expert review' rather than leaving it implicit.
planned_changes.description: Document normalization constants and any assumptions.

acceptance_criteria: computed_columns.csv and README.md are present and allow repro of normalized_score values.
Preview evidence (text or CSV) supports the claims in release_decision.md without needing workbook parsing.
actions: Include computed_columns.csv and a short README.md describing which CSV columns map to which workbook cells and formulas.
Re-run the submission flow and confirm artifact previews parse; if preview tooling still cannot parse XLSX, ensure verifier can use the CSV alone to validate claims.
Update metadata in release_decision.md to state that computed_columns.csv can be used for automated verification.
planned_changes.description: Provide verification-friendly exports and update submission metadata.

acceptance_criteria: Submission metadata contains a single authoritative final_score and passed flag.
Rubric item ri_1 is either passed or failed consistently across metadata fields; if failed, the reason is documented.
actions: In any grader/metadata outputs produced by the worker tools, ensure final_score and passed flags are set consistently and document how semantic scoring aggregates deterministic precheck and model judgments.
Avoid duplicating raw output sections with conflicting values in the submission metadata files.
planned_changes.description: Ensure consistency of pass/score reporting and rubric flags.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Resolve missing explicit normalized_score formula, provide workbook formula provenance and previewable intermediate outputs, and remove internal grader/verifier inconsistencies so rubric evaluation can validate semantic correctness.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The deliverables are present and the decision report documents row-level classifications and remediation scenarios, but the submission fails the rubric because it does not include an explicit normalized risk-score (normalized_score) formula. The workbook could not be programmatically validated in preview (openpyxl unavailable), and the grader/verifier outputs contain internal inconsistencies (contradictory pass flags and rubric-item pass/fail duplication) that should be cleaned. To pass, provide the explicit normalized-score formula (in the MD and as visible, referenced cells in the XLSX), ensure workbook formulas reference embedded_packet input cells (no hidden constants), and produce a previewable export (or CSV of intermediate computations) so verifiers can validate provenance. Also fix metadata/score reporting so pass/score flags are consistent.
